title: Unboxing the Unitree G1 Edu Humanoid
transcript: Hey little puppy. Hi. The unit G1 is here. This is the G1 edu Ultimate B sent to me. A dream come true through an advisory role I have with a company called Lucky Robots which is aiming to accelerate simulated robotics training. Despite my best efforts, I have no affiliation with Unitry, but I have very badly wanted to check out both their quadripeds and now their humanoids. This particular G1 was purchased through Robo Store in the United States at the suggestion of Unitry. Apparently, they're the US distributor. It took about 1.5 months to receive the robot after purchase, but we still don't yet have the remote controller or the gantry, which the gantry is used to suspend the robot semisafely, so you can do like R&D. The remote is supposedly on the way, and then through a little bit of back and forth, the gantry is too. That is the simplest base model and also before like taxes, tariffs, all of that. that model, even that base model to your door in the United States at least, is going to be more like $24,000.
